description: Hand-curated 50-query subset of BrainBench Cat 13 (conceptual recall). Each query is a paraphrase or vocabulary substitution that would NOT match the target page via keyword/grep. A graph adapter that traverses typed edges from entity names would also miss most of these because the phrasing avoids the page title. Curated from SYNONYMS dict in eval/runner/cat13-conceptual.ts. Used by the v0.35.1.0 embedder shootout (see docs/designs/2026_05_EVAL_PLAN.md in gbrain).
curation_rule: include a query only if a competent grep/BM25 adapter would likely miss it but a semantic embedder would likely find it. Test: does the query share <30% of its content words with the target page's title and compiled_truth.

queries.text: unscalable founder work that doesn't generalize
queries.inclusion_reason: no overlap with title 'do things that don't scale'; pure paraphrase

queries.text: manual white-glove onboarding for the earliest users
hand-crafted early traction
when the product pulls users toward it instead of being pushed
queries.inclusion_reason: metaphorical phrasing avoids the literal term PMF

queries.text: the thing founders chase before scale
queries.inclusion_reason: indirect reference, no overlap with title

queries.text: market pull versus market push
hands-on founder involvement at every layer
queries.inclusion_reason: rephrases founder-mode without using the term

queries.text: the opposite of professional management
deep founder ownership of execution details
queries.inclusion_reason: actionable phrasing instead of named concept

queries.text: AI agents collaborating to complete tasks
agent-to-agent delegation patterns
multi-agent orchestration in production systems
per-customer profitability after acquisition costs
queries.inclusion_reason: definition-style; avoids the term unit economics

queries.text: CAC versus LTV math
metered billing where you pay only for what you consume
consumption pricing model
industry-specific software for one narrow vertical
specialized enterprise tools that only work in one domain
broadly-applicable developer infrastructure
queries.inclusion_reason: describes horizontal-api scope without the term

queries.text: cross-industry API platform
large pretrained base models that everyone builds on
general-purpose LLMs trained on broad corpora
taking a base model and teaching it new tricks
domain adaptation of a generalist model
what it costs to actually run the model in production
runtime LLM cost per request
how fast the product has to feel to the user
speed SLA constraints in real-time apps
grounding LLMs in external private documents
injecting your own context into model calls
OSS-led GTM where the free tier creates pipeline
free-then-paid open-core strategy
building bottoms-up developer mindshare
developer advocacy as a function
grassroots adoption strategy driven by users
self-serve-first growth motion
freemium-driven user expansion
six-figure-contract B2B selling motion
selling directly to the C-suite executives
models that handle vision plus text plus audio together
cross-modality AI systems beyond text-only
products where the language model is the product itself
AI as a core primitive instead of a feature bolted on
finance APIs powering other companies' apps
invisible financial infrastructure inside non-fintech products
retention sliced by signup month
revenue riding entirely on one whale account
margin improvement as the business gets bigger
how sticky the ARR actually is over multi-year horizons
what fraction of a customer's total spend the product captures
queries.inclusion_reason: definitional paraphrase; avoids both 'wallet' and 'share' to keep grep adapter blind
